findings.title: Deploy script target configuration is not asserted by the deploy test
findings.suggested_fix: Extend test_DeployConfiguredRouterRequiresLaunchableManifest to iterate FameRouterFixtureManifest.requiredVenueTargetCount() on the returned router and assert every required family and target is enabled, including NativeWrap/WETH and AerodromeV2. Also assert feeRecipient and feePpm on the deployed router so deployConfiguredRouter is covered as the production materialization path, not only as an owner-transfer smoke test.

findings.title: Production materialization rejection branches are only partially tested
findings.suggested_fix: Add router-ts tests for deadline <= now, nonzero NativeWrap leg minimums, and zero quote-derived minimums on non-NativeWrap swap legs. The existing tests cover long-lived deadlines, zero final minimums, and missing leg-minimum count, but not these new guard branches that protect production route materialization.

findings.title: Public config secret-safety policy has no automated assertion
findings.suggested_fix: Add a lightweight test or verification script that scans config/fame-public.env for forbidden secret-shaped variable names and values, such as RPC URLs, PRIVATE_KEY, MNEMONIC, API_KEY, SNIPE keys, and 64-hex private-key literals. Wire it into the local verification path so the documented Doppler/public-config split is enforced, not just stated in comments.

residual_risks: The fork suites provide the meaningful Base, NativeWrap, and Aerodrome V2 execution proof, but they depend on BASE_RPC and will skip most route execution tests when that secret is absent. The launch-gate test intentionally fails when the manifest is launchable and BASE_RPC is missing, so CI must include that test path to avoid silent fork coverage loss.
I did not run tests because this review was requested with read-only commands only. The findings are based on static inspection of the diff artifact, source, and tests.
testing_gaps: No deploy-script test proves deployConfiguredRouter enables every manifest-required venue family/target on the returned router.
No router-ts production materialization tests cover expired deadlines, nonzero NativeWrap production minimums, or zero non-NativeWrap leg minimums.
No automated secret-safety assertion currently enforces that config/fame-public.env remains public-only.
Aerodrome V2 has unit and fork coverage, but the pure unit tests still rely on a permissive mock venue; the real ABI/factory behavior is only proven by the Base fork suite.
